title: Authorized medical devices for uses related to COVID-19: List of products no longer authorized - Canada.ca
content:
## ARCHIVED Authorized medical devices for uses related to COVID-19: List of products no longer authorized
- List of authorized testing devices
- List of authorized medical devices other than testing
- List of medical devices for expanded use in relation to COVID-19
- List of medical devices for exceptional import and sale
- List of products no longer authorized
This is an archived page. Please refer to the current version of the List of medical devices no longer authorized.
- What is included in this list
- Reasons for cancelling an authorization

## What is included in this list
Authorizations may be revoked for some medical devices that were authorized under the:
- first interim order for importing and selling medical devices during COVID-19
- second interim order for importing and selling medical devices during COVID-19
- third interim order for importing and selling medical devices during COVID-19 or
- Part 1.1 of the Medical Devices Regulations (MDR)

## Reasons for cancelling an authorization
As per section 68.21 of the MDR, the Minister may cancel the authorization for importing or selling a COVID-19 medical device if:
- the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that: the authorization holder has contravened the Act or the Regulations the risks outweigh the benefits associated with the device having regard to the uncertainties relating to the benefits and risks and the UPHN or absence of UPHN for the device the risks the authorization holder does not have an adequate quality management system in place the health or safety of patients, users or other persons may be unduly affected by the device or the authorization holder failed to comply with the terms and conditions of the authorization
- the authorization holder has contravened the Act or the Regulations
- the risks outweigh the benefits associated with the device having regard to the uncertainties relating to the benefits and risks and the UPHN or absence of UPHN for the device the risks
- the uncertainties relating to the benefits and risks and
- the UPHN or absence of UPHN for the device the risks
- the authorization holder does not have an adequate quality management system in place
- the health or safety of patients, users or other persons may be unduly affected by the device or
- the authorization holder failed to comply with the terms and conditions of the authorization
- the authorization for sale of a COVID-19 medical device that was issued by a foreign regulatory authority is suspended or cancelled
- the authorization holder does not submit additional information, documents or material to the Minister within the time limit specified in the request or
- the authorization holder does not meet additional requirements within the prescribed timelines after their medical device is no longer on the UPHN list
An authorization is cancelled when:
- the authorization holder of a Class I COVID-19 medical device has been issued an MDEL under Part 1 of the MDR
- the authorization holder of a Class II, III or IV COVID-19 medical device has been issued an MDL under Part 1 of the MDR or
- the authorization holder either informs the Minister that they are no longer selling the device in Canada or requests that the authorization be cancelled
We will implement compliance and enforcement measures if a COVID-19 medical device authorized for importation or sale under Part 1.1 of the MDR is in non-compliance.
For more information, please consult the following guidance document:
- Medical devices for use in relation to COVID-19
